sourceMovie.title: Maleficent
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: Maleficent (2014) stuck because it commits to its fantasy premise without apologizing—viewers remember set pieces and momentum as much as plot. Robert Stromberg's control of tone keeps the ride coherent. The fantasy and adventure blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: Stardust
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Maleficent clicked for you, Stardust (2007) is a strong next stop: both trade in fantasy and adventure, with earned warmth without mush. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

recommendations.title: The Little Mermaid
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Maleficent clicked for you, The Little Mermaid (2023) is a strong next stop: both trade in fantasy and adventure, with earned warmth without mush. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

faq.question: What should I watch after Maleficent?
faq.answer: Try Stardust, The Little Mermaid, Beauty and the Beast—each shares DNA with Maleficent while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is Maleficent?
faq.answer: Maleficent (2014) is best described as Fantasy, Adventure, Action, Family, Romance. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is Maleficent part of a franchise?
faq.answer: Yes—it's part of the "Maleficent Collection" storyline. You can enjoy it standalone, but sequels and tie-ins build on characters and continuity established here.
